title: Boat Rental in Mallorca: Costs, Seasons and What to Know in 2026
html:
Renting a boat in Mallorca offers a brilliant way to explore the Balearic coastline, with prices starting from around 90 EUR for a small motorboat for half a day. Depart from Palma's marina or Port d'Andratx and browse verified options on BoatHire24 to secure your ideal vessel.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Mallorca?

Prices vary widely by boat type and season, with a shared tour from 50 EUR per person to a crewed yacht from 3,000 EUR per day. The main cost drivers are boat size, duration, and whether you hire a skipper. Fuel and VAT are usually extra, so check the listing details. When comparing options, consider the yacht charter Mallorca page for up-to-date availability and transparent pricing.

  • Shared tour: 50, 100 EUR per person for a half-day excursion around the coast.
  • Small motorboat (6m, 8m): 150, 350 EUR for a half day without skipper.
  • Catamaran full-day charter: 800, 2,500 EUR depending on size and crew.
  • Luxury crewed yacht (15m+): 3,000, 8,000 EUR per day including crew.

Where to set off in Mallorca

Mallorca has dozens of well-equipped marinas serving as departure points for boat rental. The main hubs include Palma Marina (the capital's superyacht port), Port Adriano (a modern facility near El Toro), and Port d'Andratx (a charming natural harbour in the southwest). Other popular spots are Cala d'Or on the east coast, Port de Sóller in the northwest, and Alcudia in the north. Each offers a range of boat sizes and easy access to nearby coves and islands like Cabrera.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (4, 6 people): Ideal for exploring Cala Llombards and other hidden coves, from 150 EUR for a half day.
  2. Catamaran (8, 12 people): Stable and spacious for family groups, full-day rates from 800 EUR; popular models include the Lagoon 42.
  3. Motor yacht (6, 10 people): Faster and sportier, perfect for reaching Formentor in under an hour, from 600 EUR for a half day. Check the Salty Sun Charters listing for a 40ft example.
  4. Sailboat (4, 8 people): Classic sailing experience, from 400 EUR for a half day; try a Beneteau or Sunseeker model.
  5. Luxury yacht (10+ people): Fully crewed with Azimut or Astondoa models, from 3,000 EUR per day.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Mallorca?

You need a valid boating licence to rent a boat without a skipper in Mallorca, but you can charter a crewed vessel licence-free. For licence-free rental, you can hire a skipper, many listings include this option. The operator will confirm the exact licence requirements for your nationality, so always ask before booking. BoatHire24 listings typically state whether a licence is needed.

In high season (June to September), the prevailing wind is the Embat, a south-easterly that picks up after midday. Plan your return to harbour before 15:00 to avoid choppy seas.

When is the best season for boating in Mallorca?

The best time is June through September, when sea temperatures are warm and winds are light, though July and August are peak tourist months. May and October offer quieter anchorages but cooler water. Always check the weather forecast for local winds such as the Tramontana when sailing in the north.

At a glance

  • Price range: 50 EUR to 8,000 EUR per day
  • Peak months: June, September
  • Trip length: 4 hours to full day
  • Guest capacity: 2, 30 people
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Mallorca

Can I rent a boat in Mallorca without a licence?

Yes, you can rent a boat with a skipper included, and many listings on BoatHire24 offer this option for guests without a licence.

What is the best area to charter a boat in Mallorca?

The southwest coast, around Port Adriano and Palmanova, has the highest concentration of rental boats and easy access to Cabrera National Park.

How far in advance should I book a boat in Mallorca?

For summer peak months, book at least two months ahead, especially for larger or crewed yachts.

Are pets allowed on boat rentals in Mallorca?

It depends on the operator; some permits well-behaved pets, so check the boat listing's requirements before booking.

Whether you're after a half-day adventure or a week-long cruise, Mallorca's waters are best explored by boat. Visit BoatHire24 to compare verified charters or list your boat and join the worldwide marketplace.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Mallorca and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
